title: शनि महात्म्य स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री शनि महात्म्य स्तोत्रम् ॥

सूर्यपुत्रो दीर्घदेहो विशालाक्षः शिवप्रियः।
मन्दचारः प्रसन्नात्मा पीडां हरतु मे शनिः॥१॥

छायामार्तण्डसम्भूतं यं पश्यन्ति विवस्वतः।
तं नमामि शनैश्चरं प्रणिपातार्तिनाशनम्॥२॥

सूर्यपुत्रो महाभागः छायापुत्रो महाग्रहः।
चन्डकोपी कर्मफलदो नमस्तेऽस्तु शनैश्चर॥३॥

नीलाञ्जनचयप्रख्यं रवेः पुत्रमनीकिनम्।
छायागर्भसमुद्भूतं तं नमामि शनैश्चरम्॥४॥

शनैश्चरस्य महात्म्यं यो जानाति विचक्षणः।
स एव पूजयेद्भक्त्या न तस्य ग्रहपीडनम्॥५॥

सर्वग्रहाणामधिपो ब्रह्मा विष्णुर्महेश्वरः।
ते ऽपि यत्र प्रभावेन शनिस्तत्र नमो नमः॥६॥

सूर्यं चन्द्रं मङ्गलं च बुधं गुरुं शुक्रं तथा।
राहुं केतुं ग्रहान् सर्वान् शनिः शासति सर्वदा॥७॥

ऐरावतगजारूढं शक्रं यः क्लेशयत्यहो।
तं शनैश्चरदेवेशं प्रणमामि कृताञ्जलिः॥८॥

सूर्यपुत्रदयायुक्तं शनैश्चरकृपानिधिम्।
सप्तवर्षगतं चैव साढेसातिफलप्रदम्॥९॥

क्रूरदृष्टिमहाक्रूरो मन्दमन्दगतिः शनिः।
भक्तानुग्रहकर्ता च दण्डनेता च दुष्कृताम्॥१०॥

कर्मदेवो महाज्ञानी धर्मपालो विचारकः।
शनिदेवस्य महात्म्यं न जाने को वदेद्बुधः॥११॥

श्रीमान् कुरूपो विकृतो विशालो गृध्रवाहनः।
कृष्णवर्णस्तथा क्रूरो दीनबन्धुः शनीश्वरः॥१२॥

॥ फलश्रुति ॥
शनिमाहात्म्यमेतद्धि यः पठेच्छृणुयात्तथा।
शनिपीडा न बाधेत सर्वसिद्धिमवाप्नुयात्॥

॥ इति श्री शनि महात्म्य स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shrī Śani Māhātmya Stotram ||

Sūryaputro dīrghadehho viśālākṣaḥ śivapriyaḥ.
Mandachāraḥ prasannātmā pīḍāṁ haratu me Śaniḥ.||1||

Chhāyāmārtaṇḍasambhūtaṁ yaṁ paśyanti Vivasvataḥ.
Taṁ namāmi Śanaiścharaṁ praṇipātārtināśanam.||2||

Sūryaputro mahābhāgaḥ Chhāyāputro mahāgrahaḥ.
Chaṇḍakopī karmaphaladō namaste'stu Śanaiśchara.||3||

Nīlāñjanachayaprakhyaṁ Raveḥ putramanīkinam.
Chhāyāgarbhasamudbhūtaṁ taṁ namāmi Śanaiścharam.||4||

Śanaiścharasya māhātmyaṁ yo jānāti vichakṣaṇaḥ.
Sa eva pūjayedbhaktyā na tasya grahapīḍanam.||5||

Sarvagraāhāṇāmadhipo Brahmā Viṣṇurmaheśvaraḥ.
Te'pi yatra prabhāvena Śanistatra namo namaḥ.||6||

Sūryaṁ Chandraṁ Maṅgalaṁ cha Budhaṁ Guruṁ Śukraṁ tathā.
Rāhuṁ Ketuṁ grahān sarvān Śaniḥ śāsati sarvadā.||7||

Airāvatagajārūḍhaṁ Śakraṁ yaḥ kleśayatyaho.
Taṁ Śanaiścharadeveśaṁ praṇamāmi kṛtāñjaliḥ.||8||

Sūryaputradayāyuktaṁ Śanaiścharakṛpānidhim.
Saptavarṣagataṁ chaiva sāḍhesātiphalapradam.||9||

Krūradṛṣṭimahākrūro mandamandagatiḥ Śaniḥ.
Bhaktānugrahaakartā cha daṇḍanetā cha duṣkṛtām.||10||

Karmadevo mahājñānī dharmapālo vichārakaḥ.
Śanidevasya māhātmyaṁ na jāne ko vadebudhaḥ.||11||

Śrīmān kurūpo vikṛto viśālo gṛdhravāhanaḥ.
Kṛṣṇavarṇastathā krūro dīnabandhuḥ Śanīśvaraḥ.||12||

|| Phalaśruti ||
Śanimāhātmyametaddhi yaḥ paṭhechchhṛṇuyāttathā.
Śanipīḍā na bādheta sarvasiddhimavāpnuyāt.

|| Iti Shrī Śani Māhātmya Stotraṁ sampūrṇam ||
hindiMeaning: (1) सूर्यपुत्र, दीर्घ देह, विशाल नेत्र, शिवप्रिय, मन्दगति, प्रसन्नात्मा शनि मेरी पीड़ा हरें। (2) छाया और मार्तण्ड (सूर्य) से उत्पन्न, प्रणाम करने वालों की पीड़ा नष्ट करने वाले शनैश्चर को नमस्कार। (3) सूर्यपुत्र, महाभाग, छायापुत्र, महाग्रह, चण्डकोपी, कर्मफलदाता शनैश्चर को नमस्कार। (4) नीले अञ्जन के ढेर जैसे, रवि के सेनापति पुत्र, छाया के गर्भ से उत्पन्न शनैश्चर को नमस्कार। (5) जो विचक्षण व्यक्ति शनि का महात्म्य जानता है और भक्ति से पूजा करता है, उसे ग्रहपीड़ा नहीं होती। (6) ब्रह्मा, विष्णु, महेश्वर भी जिसके प्रभाव में हैं, उस शनि को बारम्बार नमस्कार। (7) सूर्य, चन्द्र, मंगल, बुध, गुरु, शुक्र, राहु, केतु - सभी ग्रहों को शनि सदा शासित करते हैं। (8) ऐरावत पर बैठे इन्द्र को भी जो कष्ट देते हैं, उन शनिदेवेश को हाथ जोड़कर प्रणाम। (10) क्रूर दृष्टि, महाक्रूर, मन्द गति शनि भक्तों पर अनुग्रह करने वाले और दुष्कर्मियों को दण्ड देने वाले हैं। (12) श्रीमान, गृध्रवाहन, कृष्णवर्ण, क्रूर होते हुए भी दीनबन्धु शनीश्वर हैं।
benefits: Describes the supreme power and glory (Mahatmya) of Lord Shani
Understanding Shani's greatness and worshipping with devotion prevents affliction
Reveals that even Brahma, Vishnu, Shiva, and Indra are subject to Shani
Shani rewards devotees and punishes evildoers - this stotra invokes his grace
Reading or hearing removes Saturn's afflictions and grants all accomplishments
Especially powerful during Sade Sati and Shani Dasha periods
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Lord Shani Dev, Shani Mahatmya Stotram (शनि महात्म्य स्तोत्रम्) stands as a devotional hymn of praise. Attributed to Skanda Purana / Shani Mahatmya, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Regular recitation is believed to shani rewards devotees and punishes evildoers - this stotra invokes his grace, reveal that even Brahma, Vishnu, Shiva, and Indra are subject to Shani, and describe the supreme power and glory (Mahatmya) of Lord Shani. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Saturday (Shanivar) and during Shani Jayanti and Shani Amavasya. With 12 verses and a reading time of about 7 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Shani Mahatmya Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Shani Mahatmya Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The Shani Mahatmya Stotram (शनि महात्म्य स्तोत्रम्) is a 12-verse Sanskrit hymn that glorifies the supreme power, cosmic role, and karmic-judge function of Lord Shani (Saturn) — the slow-moving, blue-bodied, vulture-mounted son of Surya and Chhaya, who is regarded as the strictest dispenser of karma-phala in the Navagraha system. The opening verse Surya-putro dirgha-deho vishala-akshah Shiva-priyah, Manda-charah prasanna-atma pidam haratu me Shanih invokes Him as the long-bodied, large-eyed, slow-moving, ever-pleased Surya-putra and beloved-of-Shiva who removes affliction. The text reveals Shani's universal sovereignty — even Brahma, Vishnu, Shiva, and Indra are subject to His karmic judgment. The hymn is preserved in classical Shani-upasana tradition. (Source: Skanda Purana / Shani Mahatmya tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Shani called the strictest of all planets?
seoFaqs.answer: Shani is regarded as the supreme karma-deva because: (1) He is the slowest-moving graha — taking 30 years to circle the zodiac and 2.5 years per rashi (called Sade Sati when transiting the moon-sign and adjacent signs — total 7.5 years of intense karmic results); (2) Verse 6 declares that even Brahma, Vishnu, and Maheshwara are subject to His prabhava (influence); Verse 7 states all other grahas (Surya, Chandra, Mangal, Budha, Guru, Shukra, Rahu, Ketu) are governed by Shani; Verse 8 narrates how Shani afflicted even Indra mounted on Airavata; (3) He is Karma-phala-data (giver of karmic fruits) — neither rewarding nor punishing arbitrarily but with mathematical precision based on past actions; (4) His krura-drishti (cruel gaze) is feared because it forces the jiva to confront accumulated karmic debts. Yet verse 5 reveals the secret — those who understand Shani's mahatmya and worship Him with bhakti are protected from graha-pidana (planetary affliction). (Sources: Skanda Purana; Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra.)

seoFaqs.question: How does Shani Mahatmya differ from Dasharatha Shani Stotra and Shani Vajrapanjara?
seoFaqs.answer: All three are Shani-stotras but with different focus and authorship. (1) Shani Mahatmya Stotram (this text) — a 12-verse glorification (mahima-stotra) describing Shani's cosmic supremacy, His role as karma-judge, and His sovereignty over even the trinity. (2) Dasharatha Krita Shani Stotra — composed by King Dasharatha of Ayodhya (father of Lord Rama) when he averted Shani's transit into Rohini nakshatra to save the kingdom from drought; it lists 10 names of Shani (Konastha, Pingala, Babhru, Krishna, Roudrantaka, Yama, Saurih, Shanaishchara, Manda, Pippaladeshena) and is recited on Saturdays for direct relief. (3) Shani Vajrapanjara Stotra — narrated by Brahma in the Brahma Vaivarta Purana; a kavacha (armor) hymn protecting each part of the body from Shani's affliction. The current site's slug shani-stotram-dasharatha actually contains the Vajrapanjara version attributed to Dasharatha lineage. (Sources: Skanda Purana; Brahma Vaivarta Purana; Padma Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: What is Sade Sati and how does Shani Mahatmya help during it?
seoFaqs.answer: Sade Sati (साढ़े साती) literally means seven-and-a-half — the 7.5-year period when transit Shani occupies the 12th, 1st, and 2nd rashis from the natal Moon-sign (chandra-rashi). It is the most-feared astrological period in Vedic Jyotisha because Shani's slow gochara intensifies karmic fruits for 30 months in each phase. Verse 9 of the Shani Mahatmya specifically references sapta-varsha-gatam saadhe-sati-phala-pradam — the seven-year (7.5 year) Sade Sati fruit-giving transit. The stotra is prescribed during Sade Sati and Dhaiya (2.5-year half-Sade-Sati when Shani transits the 4th or 8th from Moon) because: (1) reciting Shani's mahima invokes His prasanna-atma (pleased-self) form rather than His wrathful form; (2) acknowledging Shani's supreme power through the stotra is itself a form of saranagati (surrender) which mitigates karmic severity; (3) the phala-shruti promises Shani-pida na badheta sarva-siddhim avapnuyat — Shani-affliction does not trouble and all-attainments are gained. (Sources: Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra Sade Sati chapter; Phaladeepika.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should Shani Mahatmya be recited for maximum benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: The Shani Mahatmya is most powerfully recited on Saturday (Shanivar — the dedicated day of Shani), Shani Jayanti (Vaishakha Amavasya — Shani's birthday celebrated especially at Shani Shingnapur Maharashtra and Tirunallar Tamil Nadu), Shani Amavasya (any Saturday falling on Amavasya — most powerful for Sade Sati relief), Shani Pradosha, and during Sade Sati / Shani Dhaiya / Shani Mahadasha or Antardasha periods. Ideal recitation procedure: bathe and wear black or dark-blue clothing (Shani's colors), light a sesame-oil (til-tel) lamp before a Shani idol or image, offer black sesame seeds (kala til), black urad dal, blue flowers (aparajita) and iron or steel items, then recite the 12 verses with focus on verse 1 (pidam haratu me Shanih) and verse 12 (dina-bandhuh Shaniswarah — friend of the destitute). Avoid eating meat, alcohol, or harming workers/servants on Saturdays. (Sources: Shani upasana paddhati; Drik Panchang Shani guidelines.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the symbolism of Shani's iconography described in verse 12?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 12 — Shriman kurupo vikrito vishalo gridhra-vahanah, krishna-varnas-tatha kruro dina-bandhuh Shaniswarah — describes Shani's iconography in apparent paradoxes: (1) Shriman (auspicious-glorious) yet kurupo (ugly) — meaning He grants prosperity but appears fierce; (2) vikrito (deformed) — referring to the limp He acquired when His mother Chhaya cursed Yama (Shani's brother) and the curse partially affected Shani; (3) vishalo (vast/expansive) — His karmic influence is universal; (4) gridhra-vahanah (riding a vulture/crow) — the bird that feeds on dead bodies symbolizes Shani as the disposer of accumulated karma; (5) krishna-varnah (dark-blue/black colored) — Shani is associated with the deep cosmic darkness that precedes new creation; (6) kruro (cruel) yet dina-bandhuh (friend of the destitute) — the most important paradox: Shani appears cruel but is actually the truest friend because He forces karmic purification leading to liberation. He punishes the proud-arrogant and protects the humble-righteous. (Sources: Shani Purana iconography; Brihat Samhita Shani chapter.)
